pick: en diciembre a las 23:59, ya sea el día 31 o un viernes
reasoning: "ya sea...o" is the standard written-register inclusive-OR; the single shared phrase "en diciembre a las 23:59" (no internal comma) unambiguously scopes both branches. The other two candidates attach "de diciembre" only to the nearest noun (Fridays or 31st), leaving the other arm month-free.

pick: los lunes a las 09:30, en los segundos 5 y 10
reasoning: Natural day→time→subsecond order; "a las 09:30" fixes the minute precisely without any "de cada minuto" leakage. The inverted form (candidate 2) is register-awkward; "de las 09:30" in candidate 3 is a strained preposition governing a time expression.

pick: de lunes a viernes, de las 09:00 a las 17:30, cada 15 segundos del minuto 30
reasoning: "cada 15 segundos del minuto 30" uses the genitive to anchor the seconds firmly inside minute 30, eliminating any floating-cadence reading. Candidate 3 ("en el minuto 30 cada 15 segundos") is structurally looser; candidate 2 front-loads the cadence in a way that invites misreading.

pick: a las 09:00, 17:00, 18:00 y 19:00, cada minuto del 0 al 30
reasoning: Placing the hours first establishes them as the restrictive frame before the minute window, preventing a cross-hour range (09:00:00 through 19:30) or additive misread. Candidates 2 and 3 front-load the minute range and then restrict it, which is the weaker reading order for disambiguating scope.
